english: I. sbst., resp. bka-čád (bka' chad), punishment; the preceding genit., contrary to our usage, is the genit. of the punishing person, thus: rgyál-poí čád-pa (rgyal po'i chad pa) a punishment of the magistrates, i.e. a punishment decreed or inflicted by the magistrates, frq.; seldom, if ever, genit. of the punished action, and never that of the punished person. In classical language the usual construction of the words is the following: čád-pas gčód-pa (chad pas gcod pa) to punish, mi źig-la (mi zhig la) somebody, ...pas or ...pai pyir (pa'i phyir) for having...; in more recent literature: čád-pa gčód-pa (chad pa gcod pa) Thgr., Glr.; čád-pa tób-pa (chad pa thob pa) 1. to receive the fine incurred by another 2. to suffer punishment, to pay a fine; ṅá-la čád-pa póg (nga la chad pa phog) punishment is inflicted on me, I am punished. II. 1. to promise, e.g. bkắ-las mi agál-bar (bka' las mi 'gal bar) to obey. -- 2. v. sub ačád-pa ('chad pa). III. adj. begotten, born, descended from; the Tibetans are sbreu daṅ sriṅ-mo-nas (spre'u dang srin mo nas) (or las) čád-pa (chad pa) the offspring of a monkey and a Rakshasi Glr.; šá-nas čád-paí bu (sha nas chad pa'i bu) a full child Glr.
1. rent, torn, worn-out, ragged, tattered, sgyí-gu čád-po (sgyi gu chad po) a leaky purse. -- 2. a limited time, a term Sch.
a written contract; čad-mál-gyi yí-ge (chad mal gyi yi ge) Glr. id.
not to obtain the things hoped for, to be disappointed Sch.
1. a limited time, a term. -- 2. a time-purchase Sch. -- 3. an agreement Tar.
also čan-túg (chan thug) Sch., boiled corn or barley etc.; abras-čán ('bras chan) rice-pap, nas-čán (nas chan) barley-pap.
a pair of scissors, but the common people know only shears, which are for various purposes; the scissors mentioned in surgical books are prob. of a nicer construction.

resp. and eleg. for ču (chu) 1. water, dri-čáb (dri chab) scented water; sṅa-čáb (snga chab), pyi-čáb (phyi chab), water which at the beginning and close of the meetings in the large monasteries is handed round, and of which every one present takes a few drops on his tongue, as a symbol of purification, in place of the original ablutions. -- 2. for other fluids, as spyan-čáb (spyan chab) tears, źal-čáb (zhal chab) spittle, gsaṅ-čáb (gsang chab), or čab-gsáṅ (chab gsang) urine, ba-čab (ba chab) cow's urine (so with the Hindoos in Lh., the cow being to them a sacred animal). -- 3. in some compounds: power, dominion, authority. -- čab-rkyáṅ (chab rkyang) brass can, brass-(tea) pot with a long spout for pouring out tea, W.; also n. for Tibet, perh. on account of the large consumption of tea there. -- čab-kúṅ (chab khung) privy Cs. -- čab-sgó (chab sgo) door, čab-sgo-pa (chab sgo pa) door-keeper, porter. -- *čab-dá* (*chab da*) (spelling dubious) a wooden pail, of a similar shape as čab-rkyáṅ (chab rkyang) W. -- čab-bróm (chab brom), čab-róm (chab rom) ice. -- čub-blúg (chub blug) C. a vessel for rinsing one's mouth with water. čab-míg (chab mig) eleg. for ču-mig (chu mig) fountain, spring. -- čab-tsód (chab tshod) eleg. a watch, a clock. -- čab-aóg (chab 'og) what is subjected to a person's sway, territory, dominion etc., čab-aóg-tu sdú-ba (chab 'og tu sdu ba) to subject; čab-aóg-gi rgyál-po (chab 'og gi rgyal po) a vassal, feudal tenant Trig.; čab-aóg-pa (chab 'og pa), also čab-abáṅs (chab 'bangs) one owing allegiance to a sovereign, a subject. -- čab-šóg (chab shog) Cs. eleg. for letter, diploma etc. -- čab-sér (chab ser) eleg. for ču-sér (chu ser) matter, pus.
W., C., also Mil., lid, valve; buckle, clasp, čáb-tse (chab tse), or čáb-rtse (chab rtse) C. id.
Lex. čabs-gčíg (chabs gcig) Sch. = tabs-gčíg (thabs gcig) together.

in čám-la abébs-pa (cham la 'bebs pa) Lex. w.e.; Sch.: to throw down, to cause to lie down; to subdue, subject; to spend, consume, to have done with; by this last signification it would be a syn. to zín-pa (zin pa), and the circumstance that čams (chams) is used in Balti as an auxiliary vb. of the pf. tense agrees with that supposition, e.g. *zan zós-se čams* (*zan zos se chams*) I have done eating, == zos zin (zos zin) B.
1. cold (in the head), catarrh; sne-čám (sne cham) id.; gre-čám (gre cham) catarrh in the throat, bronchial catarrh; glo-čám (glo cham) catarrh in the lungs; rims-čám (rims cham) an infect- <
